subject: Public Health
title: Water Quality Monitoring
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Water Quality Monitoring.

hook.text: You can't see most water contaminants—testing is the only way to know if water is truly safe.

buildup.text: E. coli testing is the standard indicator—its presence signals fecal contamination and disease risk.

discovery.text: Real-time sensors can now continuously monitor pH, turbidity, chlorine levels, and microbial contamination.

twist.text: In many developing countries, water is tested only once—at the source—but contamination often occurs during transport.

climax.text: Citizen science programs are empowering communities to test their own water—closing the monitoring gap.

punchline.text: What you can't see in water can kill you. Testing makes the invisible visible.

quiz.question: What is the standard indicator organism for testing water contamination?
